Patch Set 3:

I don't think it is a good idea to import another project into the lustre source tree. We did that for libsysio and that was a mistake.

Patch Set 3:

A valid concern. That is a lot of code. I need to transfer stripe info from an application to the copytool. JSON is a perfect candidate, and leaves room for extension and private data. However there is no json library in standard RHEL, so the only solution I found is to integrate one.

Patch Set 3:

The closest thing to a JSON library on most platforms is json-glib-devel. Only distro where it is missing is RHEL6.5

commit.subject: LU-6246 utils: add CCAN JSON parser / emitter from
commit.message:
LU-6246 utils: add CCAN JSON parser / emitter from

Added CCAN JSON, from
https://ccodearchive.net/tarballs/with-deps/json.tar.bz2.

     0 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/LICENSE -> ../../licenses/BSD-MIT
  3046 2014-11-07 22:24 ccan/json/_info
 29298 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/json.c
  3492 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/json.h
   242 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/test/common.h
  4518 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/test/run-construction.c
  1726 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/test/run-decode-encode.c
  2433 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/test/run-stringify.c
   974 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/test/run-validate.c
  4267 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/test/test-strings
   967 2012-11-17 22:39 ccan/json/test/test-strings-reencoded
  1023 2011-11-13 17:30 licenses/BSD-MIT

Imported as-is, except for licenses/BSD-MIT that was moved over
ccan/json/LICENSE, leaving only the ccan/ directory.

Signed-off-by: frank zago <fzago@cray.com>
Change-Id: I0d4a05fa29f5611892d392c8230068c725eb0c9c
